response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior full-stack engineer with genuine low-latency and distributed-systems exposure, but the profile diverges sharply from the role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. Most of the career centers on web/full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than cloud compute platforms, and there is no demonstrated formal technical-leadership experience managing cross-team priorities or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers at the level this role demands.
strengths: Real high-scale, low-latency systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching/workshops
Broad technical range across backend, infrastructure tooling, Kubernetes deployments, and DevOps setup
concerns: Falls well short of the 8-year C++ requirement, a hard minimum for this role
No formal technical-lead role owning strategy, priorities, and deliverables across teams
Career focus is web/product engineering, not cloud compute platforms or hardware/storage architecture

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal technical leadership track record
